back_translation: Keep the month of Abib and make a Passover to the Lord your God, for in the month of Abib the Lord your God brought you out from Egypt by night.
And you shall sacrifice the Passover to the Lord your God, flock and herd, in the place that the Lord will choose to cause His name to dwell there.

You shall not eat with it leaven; seven days you shall eat with it unleavened bread, the bread of affliction, for in haste you came out from the land of Egypt, so that you may remember the day of your going out from the land of Egypt all the days of your life.
And no leaven shall be seen with you in all your border for seven days, and none of the meat that you sacrifice in the evening on the first day shall remain until morning.
You shall not be able to sacrifice the Passover in one of your gates that the Lord your God is giving to you.
But to the place that the Lord your God will choose to cause His name to dwell there, you shall sacrifice the Passover in the evening, as the sun goes down, the time of your going out from Egypt.
And you shall cook and eat in the place that the Lord your God will choose in it, and you shall turn in the morning and go to your tents.
Six days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day a solemn assembly to the Lord your God; you shall not do work.
Seven weeks you shall count for yourself; from the beginning of the sickle in the standing grain you shall begin to count seven weeks.
And you shall make the Feast of Weeks to the Lord your God, the measure of the freewill offering of your hand that you give, as the Lord your God has blessed you.

And you shall rejoice before the Lord your God, you and your son and your daughter and your manservant and your maidservant, and the Levite who is in your gates, and the foreigner and the fatherless and the widow who are in your midst, in the place that the Lord your God will choose to cause His name to dwell there.
And you shall remember that a slave you were in Egypt, and you shall keep and do these statutes.
The Feast of Tabernacles you shall make for yourself seven days, in your gathering from your threshing floor and from your winepress.

And you shall rejoice in your feast, you and your son and your daughter and your manservant and your maidservant, and the Levite and the foreigner and the fatherless and the widow who are in your gates.

Seven days you shall feast to the Lord your God in the place that the Lord will choose, for the Lord your God will bless you in all your produce and in all the work of your hands, and you shall be only joyful.

Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the face of the Lord your God in the place that He will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread, and at the Feast of Weeks, and at the Feast of Tabernacles. And he shall not appear before the face of the Lord empty.
Each man according to the gift of his hand, according to the blessing of the Lord your God that He has given to you.
Judges and officers you shall give for yourself in all your gates that the Lord your God is giving to you for your tribes, and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.
You shall not turn aside justice; you shall not recognize faces; and you shall not take a bribe, for the bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and twists the words of the righteous.
Justice, justice you shall pursue, so that you may live and possess the land that the Lord your God is giving to you.
You shall not plant for yourself an Asherah, any tree, beside the altar of the Lord your God that you will make for yourself.
And you shall not set up for yourself a pillar, which the Lord your God hates.
